2605.25850.title: TIAR: Trajectory-Informed Advantage Reweighting for LLM Abstention Learning
2605.25850.paper_summary: This paper investigates large language model (LLM) abstention learning, specifically using ternary reward, which incentivize truthfulness in large language models. This paper extends that idea by moving from a ternary reward to a Trajectory-Informed advantage reweighting, dynamically re-weights the abstention reward during Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) training. The objective of this work focuses on abstention learning instead of improving truthfulness, serving as an exploration into hallucination reduction. The novelty of this paper lies in methodological innovation, advantage re-weighting, and benchmark selection. Leveraging GRPO's multiple trajectories as a natural abstention signal, this method uses a reward signal to explore knowledge boundaries and encourage consistency. By demonstrating that trajectories can be used as a confidence indicator of the policy relative to the query, they are then used to dynamically calculate the abstention advantage. AbstentionBench is used as the evaluation benchmark, as this work aims to contribute to the field of abstention learning. All datasets on the benchmark were tested against this method and various baselines. Empirical results demonstrate that TIAR achieves state-of-the-art abstention F1 scores across five of six evaluation categories, outperforming the static ternary baseline on 17 of 31 benchmark datasets while fully preserving baseline accuracy.
2605.25850.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文探討大型語言模型（LLM）的棄權學習，特別是使用三元獎勵，這種獎勵能激勵大型語言模型的真實性。這篇論文通過將三元獎勵轉變為基於軌跡的信息優勢重加權，擴展了這一理念，並在群體相對政策優化（GRPO）訓練過程中動態地重新加權棄權獎勵。這項工作的目標集中在棄權學習上，而不是提高真實性，作為對幻覺減少的一種探索。這篇論文的新穎性體現在方法創新、優勢重加權和基準選擇上。利用GRPO的多條軌跡作為自然的棄權信號，這種方法使用獎勵信號來探索知識邊界並鼓勵一致性。通過展示軌跡可以作為相對於查詢的政策信心指標，然後用它們來動態計算棄權優勢。AbstentionBench被用作評估基準，因為這項工作旨在為棄權學習領域做出貢獻。基準上的所有數據集都針對這種方法和各種基線進行了測試。實證結果顯示，TIAR在六個評估類別中的五個類別中達到了最先進的棄權F1分數，在31個基準數據集中超越了靜態三元基線的17個，同時完全保留了基線的準確性。
2605.25850.author: Muyu Pan et.al.
2605.25850.authors: Muyu Pan, Shu Zhao, Nan Zhang, Philip Shin, Varun Parekh, Vijaykrishnan Narayanan, Rui Zhang
